creator: _jkrow
caption:
I did not expect a state this small to fit all of this into one weekend 🌊🚣‍♀️🏁

👉 Save this for your East Coast bucket list if you only have a few days of PTO and want a trip that packs WAY more into one weekend than you’d expect! 

@visitdelaware is a state you can drive across in just a few hours, but still kayak through bald cypress forests, experience NASCAR weekend at Dover Motor Speedway, hike coastal trails, relax on the beach AND do some tax free shopping in beautiful small towns…all in one trip. 

There was still so much we didn’t get to do and I already want to go back!

KNOW BEFORE YOU GO:

🏎️ If you’re hoping to experience NASCAR, plan around race weekends at Dover Motor Speedway

🛶 Trap Pond is best experienced by kayak. You can rent them there or bring your own

🌊 Cape Henlopen is an easy beach stop before heading home. Bring binoculars or your telephoto lens to spot sea birds nesting and fishing along the coast!

📍 Say “DELAWARE” and I’ll send you a full itinerary!

#visitDE

transcription.text: Most people don't know that this tiny east coast state has hidden cypress forests, NASCAR, beaches, and coastal trails. This is Delaware, and for a state you can drive across in just a few hours, it is wild that all of this exists and you can do it in just one weekend trip. Here's exactly how to spend three days exploring. On day one, check into a cozy bed and breakfast, and then spend the afternoon kayaking through Trap Pond State Park. This is home to the northernmost stand of bald cypress trees in the country, and it actually feels more like Louisiana than the east coast. If you're visiting during a NASCAR weekend, spend day two with the iconic Monster Mile at Dover Motor Speedway. The race is super exciting, but even without tickets, you can still hang out in the free fan zone with food, games, merch, and fun events. Finish the day with some fried seafood at Sambo's Tavern, or grab dinner at any of the amazing restaurants in the area. For your last day, head to the coast. Visit Cape Hennepin State Park, where the Delaware Bay meets the Atlantic Ocean, and spend some time at the beach, walk the trails, and do some bird watching. If you have extra time, stop by some of Delaware's small coastal towns to take advantage of tax-free shopping before you go home. Come in Delaware, and I'll send you the full itinerary.
